memberdef.argsstring: (VertCoords vertexCoordinates, std_vector_Id_VertTag verts, Vector_MeshBuilder_VertSpan_FaceId faces, MeshBuilder.BuildSettings settings=..., func_bool_from_float cb='{}')
memberdef.name: fromFaceSoup

para.verbatim: construct mesh from TriMesh representation

memberdef.argsstring: (VertCoords vertexCoordinates, Triangulation t, MeshBuilder.BuildSettings settings=..., func_bool_from_float cb='{}')
memberdef.name: fromTriangles

para.verbatim: construct mesh from vertex coordinates and a set of triangles with given ids

memberdef.argsstring: (VertCoords vertexCoordinates, Triangulation t, std_vector_MeshBuilder_VertDuplication dups=None, MeshBuilder.BuildSettings settings=...)
memberdef.name: fromTrianglesDuplicatingNonManifoldVertices

para.verbatim: construct mesh from vertex coordinates and a set of triangles with given ids; unlike simple fromTriangles() it tries to resolve non-manifold vertices by creating duplicate vertices
compare that two meshes are exactly the same
Implicit copy constructor.

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Mesh from_, PartMapping map=..., bool rearrangeTriangles=False)
memberdef.name: addMesh

para.verbatim: appends another mesh as separate connected component(s) to this

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Mesh from_, FaceMap outFmap, VertMap outVmap=None, WholeEdgeMap outEmap=None, bool rearrangeTriangles=False)
memberdef.name: addMesh

para.verbatim: appends whole or part of another mesh as separate connected component(s) to this

memberdef.argsstring: (self, MeshPart from_, bool flipOrientation=False, std_vector_std_vector_Id_EdgeTag thisContours=..., std_vector_std_vector_Id_EdgeTag fromContours=..., PartMapping map=...)
memberdef.name: addMeshPart

para.verbatim: appends whole or part of another mesh to this joining added faces with existed ones along given contours \\param flipOrientation true means that every (from) triangle is inverted before adding
creates new point and assigns given position to it
append points to mesh and connect them returns first EdgeId of new edges
append points to mesh and connect them as closed edge loop returns first EdgeId of new edges
returns the area of given face
computes the area of given face-region (or whole mesh)

memberdef.argsstring: (self, EdgeId first, EdgeId last, std_vector_Vector3_float contourPoints)
memberdef.name: attachEdgeLoopPart

para.verbatim: append points to mesh and connect them to given edges making edge loop first point connects with first edge dest last point connects with last edge org note that first and last edge should have no left face
computes average length of an edge in this mesh
returns circumcircle diameter of given mesh triangle
returns squared circumcircle diameter of given mesh triangle

memberdef.argsstring: (self, FaceBitSet region, bool flipOrientation=False, PartMapping map=...)
memberdef.name: cloneRegion

para.verbatim: creates new mesh from given triangles of this mesh
passes through all valid vertices and finds the minimal bounding box containing all of them; if toWorld transformation is given then returns minimal bounding box in world space

passes through all given faces (or whole mesh if region == null) and finds the minimal bounding box containing all of them if toWorld transformation is given then returns minimal bounding box in world space
computes sum of cotangents of the angle in the left and right triangles opposite to given edge, consider cotangents zero for not existing triangles
returns twice the area of given face
computes the length of summed directed double areas of all triangles around given vertex
deletes multiple given faces, also deletes adjacent edges and vertices if they were not shared by remaining faces and not in \\param keepEdges
returns coordinates of the edge destination

given an edge between two triangular faces, computes the dihedral angle between them: 0 if both faces are in the same plane, positive if the faces form convex surface, negative if the faces form concave surface; please consider the usage of faster dihedralAngleSin(e) and dihedralAngleCos(e)

given an edge between two triangular faces, computes cosine of dihedral angle between them: 1 if both faces are in the same plane, 0 if the surface makes right angle turn at the edge, -1 if the faces overlap one another

given an edge between two triangular faces, computes sine of dihedral angle between them: 0 if both faces are in the same plane, positive if the faces form convex surface, negative if the faces form concave surface
computes the sum of directed areas for faces from given region (or whole mesh)
computes directed double area for a triangular face from its vertices
computes sum of directed double areas of all triangles around given vertex

computes discrete Gaussian curvature (or angle defect) at given vertex, which 0 in inner vertices on planar mesh parts and reaches 2*pi on needle's tip, see http://math.uchicago.edu/~may/REU2015/REUPapers/Upadhyay.pdf optionally returns whether the vertex is on boundary
computes discrete mean curvature in given vertex, measures in length^-1; 0 for planar regions, positive for convex surface, negative for concave surface
computes discrete mean curvature in given edge, measures in length^-1; 0 for planar regions, positive for convex surface, negative for concave surface
computes the center of given edge
returns Euclidean length of the edge
returns squared Euclidean length of the edge (faster to compute than length)
computes and returns the lengths of all edges in the mesh
returns a point on the edge: origin point for f=0 and destination point for f=1
computes coordinates of point given as edge and relative position on it
returns line segment of given edge
returns vector equal to edge destination point minus edge origin point
computes bounding box and returns its center
computes center of mass considering that density of all triangles is the same
computes average position of all valid mesh vertices

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Vector3f point, MeshProjectionResult res, float maxDistSq=3.4028234663852886e+38, FaceBitSet region=None, AffineXf3f xf=None)
memberdef.name: findClosestPoint

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Vector3f point, float maxDistSq=3.4028234663852886e+38, FaceBitSet region=None, AffineXf3f xf=None)
memberdef.name: findClosestPoint

para.verbatim: finds all mesh edges where dihedral angle is distinct from planar PI angle on at least given value

memberdef.argsstring: (self, float minSumAngle, VertBitSet region=None, func_bool_from_float cb='{}')
memberdef.name: findSpikeVertices

para.verbatim: returns vertices where the sum of triangle angles is below given threshold
returns cached aabb-tree for this mesh, creating it if it did not exist in a thread-safe manner
returns cached aabb-tree for this mesh, but does not create it if it did not exist
returns cached aabb-tree for points of this mesh, creating it if it did not exist in a thread-safe manner
returns cached aabb-tree for points of this mesh, but does not create it if it did not exist
returns the bounding box containing all valid vertices (implemented via getAABBTree()) this bounding box is insignificantly bigger that minimal box due to AABB algorithms precision
returns one of three face edges, closest to given point
returns one of three face edges, closest to given point
returns one of three face vertices, closest to given point
returns one of three face vertices, closest to given point
returns cached dipoles of aabb-tree nodes for this mesh, creating it if it did not exist in a thread-safe manner
returns cached dipoles of aabb-tree nodes for this mesh, but does not create it if it did not exist

memberdef.argsstring: (self, EdgeId e, Vector3f v0, Vector3f v1, Vector3f v2)
memberdef.name: getLeftTriPoints

para.verbatim: returns three points of left face of e: v0 = orgPnt( e ), v1 = destPnt( e )
returns three points of left face of e: res[0] = orgPnt( e ), res[1] = destPnt( e )
returns the plane containing given triangular face with normal looking outwards

memberdef.argsstring: (self, FaceId f, Vector3f v0, Vector3f v1, Vector3f v2)
memberdef.name: getTriPoints

para.verbatim: returns three points of given face
returns three points of given face
returns the amount of memory this object occupies on heap

computes directed area of the hole specified by one of its edges with no valid left face (left is hole); if the hole is planar then returned vector is orthogonal to the plane pointing outside and its magnitude is equal to hole area
computes the perimeter of the hole specified by one of its edges with no valid left face (left is hole)
invalidates caches (aabb-trees) after any change in mesh geometry or topology \\param pointsChanged specifies whether points have changed (otherwise only topology has changed)

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Vector3f pt, float windingNumberThreshold=0.5, float beta=2)
memberdef.name: isOutside

para.verbatim: computes whether a point (pt) is located outside the object surrounded by this mesh using generalized winding number \\param beta determines the precision of winding number computation: the more the better, recommended value 2 or more

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Vector3f pt, MeshProjectionResult proj, FaceBitSet region=None)
memberdef.name: isOutsideByProjNorm

para.verbatim: computes cotangent of the angle in the left( e ) triangle opposite to e, and returns 0 if left face does not exist
computes directed double area of left triangular face of given edge
computes triangular face normal from its vertices
computes unit vector that is both orthogonal to given edge and to the normal of its left triangle, the vector is directed inside left triangle
reflects the mesh from a given plane
computes triangular face normal from its vertices
computes normal in a vertex using sum of directed areas of neighboring triangles
computes normal in three vertices of p's triangle, then interpolates them using barycentric coordinates and normalizes again; this is the same normal as in rendering with smooth shading
returns coordinates of the edge origin
tightly packs all arrays eliminating lone edges and invalid faces, vertices and points

memberdef.argsstring: (self, FaceMap outFmap, VertMap outVmap=None, WholeEdgeMap outEmap=None, bool rearrangeTriangles=False)
memberdef.name: pack

para.verbatim: tightly packs all arrays eliminating lone edges and invalid faces, vertices and points, reorder all faces, vertices and edges according to given maps, each containing old id -> new id mapping

packs tightly and rearranges vertices, triangles and edges to put close in space elements in close indices \\param preserveAABBTree whether to keep valid mesh's AABB tree after return (it will take longer to compute and it will occupy more memory)
computes the sum of absolute projected area of faces from given region (or whole mesh) as visible if look from given direction

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Vector3f point, PointOnFace res, float maxDistSq=3.4028234663852886e+38, FaceBitSet region=None, AffineXf3f xf=None)
memberdef.name: projectPoint

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Vector3f point, MeshProjectionResult res, float maxDistSq=3.4028234663852886e+38, FaceBitSet region=None, AffineXf3f xf=None)
memberdef.name: projectPoint

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Vector3f point, float maxDistSq=3.4028234663852886e+38, FaceBitSet region=None, AffineXf3f xf=None)
memberdef.name: projectPoint

para.verbatim: computes angle-weighted sum of normals of incident faces of given vertex (only (region) faces will be considered); the sum is normalized before returning
computes normalized half sum of face normals sharing given edge (only (region) faces will be considered);

returns pseudonormal in corresponding face/edge/vertex for signed distance calculation as suggested in the article "Signed Distance Computation Using the Angle Weighted Pseudonormal" by J. Andreas Baerentzen and Henrik Aanaes, https://backend.orbit.dtu.dk/ws/portalfiles/portal/3977815/B_rentzen.pdf unlike normal( const MeshTriPoint & p ), this is not a smooth function

memberdef.argsstring: (self, VertId v, bool angleWeigted, FaceBitSet region=None, UndirectedEdgeBitSet creases=None)
memberdef.name: quadraticForm

para.verbatim: requests the removal of unused capacity

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Vector3f pt, MeshProjectionResult proj, FaceBitSet region=None)
memberdef.name: signedDistance

memberdef.argsstring: (self, Vector3f pt, float maxDistSq, FaceBitSet region=None)
memberdef.name: signedDistance

memberdef.argsstring: (self, EdgeId e, Vector3f newVertPos, FaceBitSet region=None, phmap_flat_hash_map_Id_FaceTag_Id_FaceTag new2Old=None)
memberdef.name: splitEdge

para.verbatim: split given edge on two parts: dest(returned-edge) = org(e) - newly created vertex, org(returned-edge) = org(e-before-split), dest(e) = dest(e-before-split) \\details left and right faces of given edge if valid are also subdivided on two parts each; the split edge will keep both face IDs and their degrees, and the new edge will have new face IDs and new faces are triangular; if left or right faces of the original edge were in the region, then include new parts of these faces in the region \\param new2Old receive mapping from newly appeared triangle to its original triangle (part to full)

memberdef.argsstring: (self, EdgeId e, FaceBitSet region=None, phmap_flat_hash_map_Id_FaceTag_Id_FaceTag new2Old=None)
memberdef.name: splitEdge

para.verbatim: same, but split given edge on two equal parts

memberdef.argsstring: (self, FaceId f, Vector3f newVertPos, FaceBitSet region=None, phmap_flat_hash_map_Id_FaceTag_Id_FaceTag new2Old=None)
memberdef.name: splitFace

memberdef.argsstring: (self, FaceId f, FaceBitSet region=None, phmap_flat_hash_map_Id_FaceTag_Id_FaceTag new2Old=None)
memberdef.name: splitFace

para.verbatim: same, putting new vertex in the centroid of original triangle
computes the sum of triangle angles at given vertex; optionally returns whether the vertex is on boundary
converts vertex into edge-point representation
converts edge and 3d point into edge-point representation
converts vertex into barycentric representation
converts face id and 3d point into barycentric representation
converts face id and 3d point into barycentric representation
applies given transformation to specified vertices if region is nullptr, all valid mesh vertices are used \\snippet cpp-examples/MeshModification.dox.cpp MeshTransform
returns the centroid of given triangle
computes coordinates of point given as face and barycentric representation
returns aspect ratio of given mesh triangle equal to the ratio of the circum-radius to twice its in-radius

returns volume of the object surrounded by given region (or whole mesh if (region) is nullptr); if the region has holes then each hole will be virtually filled by adding triangles for each edge and the hole's geometrical center
for all points not in topology.getValidVerts() sets coordinates to (0,0,0)

Generated from: MR::Mesh Aliases: std_shared_ptr_Mesh, std_shared_ptr_const_Mesh
This class represents a mesh, including topology (connectivity) information and point coordinates, as well as some caches to accelerate search algorithms
